title: Plerandra reginae
intro: Plerandra reginae, commonly known as the Koa Haole, is a plant species native to the Hawaiian Islands, belonging to the family Araliaceae. The health benefits of Plerandra reginae include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a promising natural remedy for various ailments. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made with Plerandra reginae include teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to support immune function, lower blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Plerandra reginae is a species of flowering plant in the family Araliaceae. It belongs to the genus Plerandra and is native to New Caledonia. Plerandra reginae is a part of the order Apiales and the class Magnoliopsida. The class Magnoliopsida is a subclass of the larger group known as the angiosperms. Plerandra reginae's classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the plant kingdom.
